zh.Luxembourg,6980,,Wallonie,BELGIQUE,La Ro: 比利時盧布格拉 洛--亞登

zh.French Flanders, France: 法國福藍德 福藍薩
zh.La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxembourg, Belgium: 比利時盧布格拉 洛--亞登
zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathilde van Limburg於1078年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇賀爾左圖姆 盧森堡時，她的父親Hendrik I van Limburg18歲，母親Adelheid von Pottenstein21歲。她於1116年與Henri I de Namur結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1148年在比利時盧布格拉 洛--亞登享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mathilde van Limburg于1078年出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇賀爾左圖姆 盧森堡时，她的父亲Hendrik I van Limburg18岁，母亲Adelheid von Pottenstein21岁。她于1116年与Henri I de Namur结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1148年在比利时盧布格拉 洛--亞登享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Limburg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places so named, the largest being in Hesse.

Dutch (also Van Limburg): habitational name for someone from the province of Limburg or from several places so named, like Limburg in the Belgian province of Liège or from Limbourg in Haut-Rhin, France, all named with the ancient Germanic elements lindo ‘lime tree’ or ‘dragon’ + burg ‘fortified place’.

nameMeanings.name: Mathilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Mathilde van Limburg was born in 1078, in Luxemburg, Holy Roman Empire, her father, Hendrik I van Limburg, was 18 and her mother, Adelheid von Pottenstein, was 21. She married Henri I de Namur in 1116. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1148, in La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxembourg, Belgium, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathilde van Limburg est née en 1078, à Luxembourg, Saint-Empire romain, son père, Hendrik I van Limburg, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Adelheid von Pottenstein, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Henri I de Namur en 1116. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1148, à La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxembourg, Belgique, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mathilde van Limburg 1078 in Herzogtum Luxemburg, Heiliges Römisches Reich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hendrik I van Limburg 18 und ihre Mutter Adelheid von Pottenstein 21. Sie hat 1116 Henri I de Namur geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1148 im Alter von 70 Jahren in La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxemburg, Belgien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde van Limburg è nata il nel 1078 nel Herzogtum Luxemburg, Heiliges Römisches Reich, sua padre, Hendrik I van Limburg, aveva 18 anni e sua madre, Adelheid von Pottenstein, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Henri I de Namur nel 1116. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1148, nel La Roche-en-Ardenne, Lussemburgo, Belgio, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.Luxemburg, Holy Roman Empire: ヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチハーゾグトゥム ルクセンブルグ

ja.La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxembourg, Belgium: ベルギールクセンボウルグラ ロチェ-エン-アルデン
ja.lifeSummary: Mathilde van Limburgが1078年にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチハーゾグトゥム ルクセンブルグで生まれた時，父Hendrik I van Limburgは18歳，母Adelheid von Pottensteinは21歳であった。1116年にHenri I de Namurと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1148年にベルギールクセンボウルグラ ロチェ-エン-アルデンで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mathilde van Limburg(이)가 1078년 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 허초그투 루세버그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hendrik I van Limburg(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adelheid von Pottenstein(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1116년 Henri I de Namur(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1148년 벨기에 루세보우르그 라 록헤--아르데니에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde van Limburg nasceu em 1078, em Herzogtum Luxemburg, Heiliges Römisches Reich, seu pai, Hendrik I van Limburg, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Adelheid von Pottenstein, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Henri I de Namur em 1116. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1148, em La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxemburgo, Bélgica, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathilde van Limburg родилась в 1078 г. в местности хирзогтум луксембург, хилигесь рoмищесь раич, её отцу по имени Hendrik I van Limburg было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelheid von Pottenstein, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henri I de Namur в 1116 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1148 г. в местности ла роше-ен-арденн, луксембург, Бельгия в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mathilde van Limburg nació en 1078, en Herzogtum Luxemburg, Heiliges Römisches Reich, su papá, Hendrik I van Limburg, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Adelheid von Pottenstein, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Henri I de Namur en 1116. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1148, en La Roche-en-Ardenne, Luxemburgo, Bélgica, a la edad de 70 años.
